summary: Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. (NASDAQ:AMD) is included among Billionaire Larry Robbins’ 10 Dividend Stock Picks. On June 12, Citi upgraded Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. headline: Is AMD’s £2 Billion UK AI Bet Altering The Investment Case For Advanced Micro Devices (AMD)?
summary: Earlier this month, AMD announced plans to invest up to £2.00 billion in the UK over five years, alongside new AI supercomputing collaborations with Dell Technologies, the University of Cambridge and Oriole Networks that expand national research and AI infrastructure. By anchoring cutting-edge systems like the Zenith and Sunrise AI supercomputers and backing photonic-networked inference research, AMD is embedding its GPUs and CPUs at the core of the UK’s long-term AI and high‑performance...

headline: This Week In AI Chips - Aymer AI Powers Up Texas for Next-Gen Infrastructure
summary: Aymer AI has revealed a strategic initiative to develop a 90-acre powered land bank in Texas, targeting next-generation AI and high-performance computing infrastructure. Dubbed Project Macedonia, the venture aims to provide up to 100 megawatts of power capacity through a mix of grid and behind-the-meter energy solutions, enhancing reliability and deployment speed for hyperscale and enterprise clients. The site offers advantageous access to energy infrastructure and major fiber routes,...

headline: EigenQ announces collaboration with TD SYNNEX to Advance Post-Quantum Security Readiness for AMD EPYC™ CPU-based Server Environments

headline: AT&S invests up to €2 billion in Malaysia plan to capitalise on AI boom
summary: Austrian circuit board manufacturer AT&S has announced it is investing ‌up to €2 billion ($2.3 billion) in Malaysia to ‌capitalise on the AI boom, sending its shares soaring nearly 30%.  "We ​will fully expand our site in Kulim," the company's CEO Michael Mertin told Reuters in an interview on Monday.  The company announced on Saturday it would expand capacity ‌in Kulim and ⁠raised its outlook for 2026/27, citing agreements with its customer AMD, the U.S. chipmaker, ⁠and another technology company on the back of strong demand for AI infrastructure.

content.title: AT&S invests up to €2 billion in Malaysia plan to capitalise on AI boom
content.summary: Austrian circuit board manufacturer AT&S has announced it is investing ‌up to €2 billion ($2.3 billion) in Malaysia to ‌capitalise on the AI boom, sending its shares soaring nearly 30%.  "We ​will fully expand our site in Kulim," the company's CEO Michael Mertin told Reuters in an interview on Monday.  The company announced on Saturday it would expand capacity ‌in Kulim and ⁠raised its outlook for 2026/27, citing agreements with its customer AMD, the U.S. chipmaker, ⁠and another technology company on the back of strong demand for AI infrastructure.

thumbnail.caption: Illustration picture of semiconductor chips on a circuit board

headline: Chip stocks surge, oil slides closer to $80 on US-Iran deal, Fox to acquire Roku
summary: Yahoo Finance Senior Reporter Brooke DiPalma comes on Market Domination to further discuss today's biggest headlines, including memory chip stocks like Micron Technology (MU) surging as Wall Street leans more bullish on the AI trade, oil prices (CL=F, BZ=F) moving closer to $80 per barrel on news of a US-Iran peace deal, and media giant Fox (FOX, FOXA) striking a deal to acquire Roku (ROKU) for $22 billion.
